prompt: Explain what a "closure" is in programming, in exactly one sentence. The sentence can be long.
response: A closure is a a fundamental concept in programming where a function has access to its own scope, as well as the scope of its outer functions, even when the outer functions have returned, allowing the inner function to "remember" and utilize the variables and context of the outer functions, thereby creating a self-contained, reusable block of code that can be passed around like any other object, while still maintaining a reference to its original environment.
